@@ -529,7 +529,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo.6","title":"FEATURE: What-If Scenario Explorer - Evidence Simulation","description":"# What-If Scenario Explorer - Evidence Simulation

## The Gap This Fills
Users can only see confidence updates AFTER recording evidence. But for planning purposes, they need to ask:
- "What if Test X comes back positive?"
- "What if Test Y comes back negative?"
- "Which test would be most informative?"

This helps prioritize which tests to pursue first.

## Feature Design

### Scenario Simulation
```typescript
interface WhatIfScenario {
  id: string;
  sessionId: string;
  hypothesisId: string;
  
  // Simulated evidence
  simulatedTests: {
    test: ExclusionTest;
    assumedResult: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'inconclusive';
  }[];
  
  // Projected impact
  projectedConfidence: number;
  confidenceDelta: number;
  
  // Analysis
  mostInformativeTest: string;
  worstCaseConfidence: number;
  bestCaseConfidence: number;
}
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Single Test Simulation
Click on any queued test and see:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  WHAT-IF: Natural Experiment (Algorithm Change)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  Current confidence: 72%                                    │
│                                                             │
│  If result SUPPORTS hypothesis:                             │
│  └─> Confidence: 72% → 81% (+9%)                           │
│                                                             │
│  If result CHALLENGES hypothesis:                           │
│  └─> Confidence: 72% → 43% (-29%)  ⚠️ Major impact        │
│                                                             │
│  If result INCONCLUSIVE:                                    │
│  └─> Confidence: 72% → 72% (±0%)                           │
│                                                             │
│  This test is HIGHLY DISCRIMINATIVE                        │
│  The asymmetry shows challenging evidence has 3x the       │
│  impact of supporting evidence (as it should).             │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 2. Multi-Test Scenarios
Build a scenario with multiple assumed results:
```
Scenario: "Pessimistic Case"
├── Test 1: Algorithm Change → Challenges
├── Test 2: Cross-cultural → Challenges  
└── Test 3: Dose-response → Supports

Result: 72% → 28% (Hypothesis severely weakened)
```

#### 3. Test Comparison
Side-by-side comparison of which tests are most informative:
```
Test Comparison (by information gain)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Test                        | Max Impact | Recommend
Algorithm Change            | ±29%       | ★★★★★
Cross-cultural              | ±18%       | ★★★★☆
Dose-response              | ±12%       | ★★★☆☆
Temporal sequence          | ±8%        | ★★☆☆☆
```

#### 4. Decision Support
"Based on what-if analysis, you should prioritize:
1. Algorithm Change test (highest discrimination)
2. Cross-cultural test (second highest)

The dose-response test is low value — even a positive result won't tell you much."

### Integration Points
- Test Queue → Enables what-if for queued tests
- Evidence Ledger → Uses same update algorithm
- Session Dashboard → Shows what-if button

## Why This is Critical
Without what-if:
- Users don't know which tests to prioritize
- They might run low-value tests first
- The test queue becomes unordered

With what-if:
- Rational prioritization of tests
- Understanding of asymmetric evidence impact
- Better allocation of research effort

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single test what-if simulation works
- [ ] Multi-test scenarios can be built
- [ ] Test comparison view shows relative value
- [ ] Recommendations based on information gain

 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo.7","title":"FEATURE: Literature Integration - Paper Search & Citation Import","description":"# Literature Integration - Paper Search & Citation Import

## The Gap This Fills
Many users will evaluate hypotheses against EXISTING research, not run new experiments. Currently:
- No way to search for relevant papers
- No integration with citation databases
- Recording literature evidence is manual
- No way to import citations

## Feature Design

### Literature Search Integration
```typescript
interface LiteratureSearch {
  query: string;
  source: 'google_scholar' | 'pubmed' | 'semantic_scholar';
  filters: {
    dateRange?: { from: number; to: number };
    openAccess?: boolean;
    citationCount?: { min: number };
  };
  
  results: PaperResult[];
}

interface PaperResult {
  title: string;
  authors: string[];
  year: number;
  abstract: string;
  citationCount: number;
  url: string;
  doi?: string;
  
  // Relevance to current hypothesis
  relevanceScore: number;
  relevanceRationale: string;
}
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Hypothesis-Aware Search
Generate search queries from hypothesis:
```
Your hypothesis: "Algorithm-driven content selection causes depression in teens"

Suggested searches:
• "social media algorithm depression adolescents"
• "content recommendation mental health teenagers"
• "Instagram TikTok depression causation"

[Search Google Scholar] [Search PubMed] [Custom Query]
```

#### 2. Paper Relevance Assessment
For each paper found, show:
- Relevance to hypothesis
- Whether it supports/challenges
- Key findings summary
- How to interpret as evidence

#### 3. Quick Evidence Recording
One-click to record paper as evidence:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Record as Evidence                                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Paper: "Social Media Use and Adolescent Mental Health:    │
│         A Systematic Review" (Smith et al., 2023)          │
│                                                             │
│  This paper reports:                                        │
│  ○ Supports your hypothesis                                 │
│  ○ Challenges your hypothesis                               │
│  ○ Inconclusive / Mixed results                             │
│                                                             │
│  Key finding: [________________________________]            │
│                                                             │
│  How does this apply to your specific hypothesis?          │
│  [                                              ]          │
│                                                             │
│  [Add to Evidence Ledger]                                   │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 4. Citation Import
Support importing from:
- DOI lookup
- BibTeX paste
- Zotero integration (future)
- Mendeley integration (future)

#### 5. Literature Review Mode
Session template focused on literature:
- Generate search strategy
- Track papers reviewed
- Systematic recording of evidence
- Summary of literature landscape

### Integration Points
- Test Queue → "Search literature for Test X"
- Evidence Ledger → Papers as evidence entries
- Research Brief → Proper citations
- Agent Tribunal → Agents can suggest papers

## Why This is Critical
Most hypothesis testing happens against literature, not original experiments. Without literature integration:
- Users have to manually search
- Recording is tedious
- Citations are incomplete

With integration:
- Streamlined literature workflow
- Proper citation tracking
- Evidence recording from papers is easy
- Research Brief has full bibliography

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search generates from hypothesis
- [ ] Results show relevance scores
- [ ] Quick recording to Evidence Ledger
- [ ] Citations properly formatted
- [ ] DOI lookup works

 {"id":"brenner_bot-nkyj","title":"Sessions UI: show lint report for latest compiled artifact","description":"On `/sessions/[threadId]`, show an at-a-glance linter summary for the latest compiled artifact, plus expandable human + JSON report.

Implementation approach:
- Re-merge all DELTA messages up to the latest COMPILED timestamp and run `lintArtifact` server-side.
- Surface a warning if additional DELTA messages arrived after the compile (stale lint).

Acceptance:
- Page renders in lab mode without throwing
- Lint summary matches compiler output for typical threads
- `cd apps/web && bun run test` passes

-{"id":"brenner_bot-nm89","title":"Tutorial Path: Multi-Agent Cockpit (10 Steps)","description":"# Purpose

Implement the Multi-Agent Cockpit tutorial path — 10 steps that teach users how to run Claude, GPT, and Gemini as a coordinated research group with persistent infrastructure.

This is the **advanced practitioner path**. It requires significant setup but delivers the highest-fidelity Brenner Loop with:
- Triangulated perspectives from different models
- Role specialization (hypothesis generator, test designer, adversarial critic)
- Persistent coordination via Agent Mail
- Compiled artifacts with delta merging

---

# Why Multi-Agent?

**Different models have different strengths**:
- GPT (Codex): Creative hypothesis generation, broad knowledge
- Claude (Opus): Careful test design, nuanced reasoning
- Gemini: Adversarial critique, finding edge cases

**Triangulation reveals blind spots**:
When three models agree, we have higher confidence.
When they disagree, we know where to dig deeper.

**Role specialization**:
Each model gets a role-specific prompt emphasizing their strengths:
- hypothesis_generator: ⊘ Level-split, ⊕ Cross-domain, ◊ Paradox-hunt
- test_designer: ✂ Exclusion-test, ⟂ Object-transpose, 🎭 Potency-check
- adversarial_critic: ΔE Exception-quarantine, † Theory-kill, ⊞ Scale-check

---

# Prerequisites

**Required**:
- Claude Max subscription (Claude Code with Opus 4.5)
- GPT Pro subscription (Codex with GPT 5.2)
- Gemini Ultra subscription (Gemini CLI with Gemini 3)
- ntm (Named Tmux Manager) installed
- Agent Mail server (local or remote)
- brenner_bot repo cloned

**Hardware**:
- Recommended: 64GB RAM VPS (like Contabo Cloud VPS 50)
- Minimum: 32GB RAM, 8+ CPU cores
- Each agent session consumes ~2-4GB RAM

**Time Investment**:
- First-time setup: ~2 hours
- Subsequent sessions: ~30-60 minutes

---

# Step-by-Step Breakdown

## Step 1: The Multi-Agent Vision (~5 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/1/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Explain why three models
- Introduce role differentiation
- Set expectations for complexity

**Key Content**:
\`\`\`markdown
# The Multi-Agent Research Cockpit

You're about to run a **research group** — three frontier AI models 
working together on your scientific question.

## Why Three Models?

Single-agent research has blind spots. The model's training biases 
shape what it sees and misses. With three models:

- **GPT** brings broad knowledge and creative hypotheses
- **Claude** brings careful reasoning and test design
- **Gemini** brings adversarial skepticism and edge-case finding

When they agree, you have triangulated confidence.
When they disagree, you've found where to dig deeper.

## The Coordination Challenge

Running three agents creates coordination problems:
- Who goes first?
- How do they share work?
- How do we merge their outputs?

This is why we need infrastructure: ntm for sessions, Agent Mail 
for messaging, the CLI for compilation.
\`\`\`

---

## Step 2: Full Prerequisites (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/2/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Verify all three CLI agents installed
- Verify ntm installed
- Check subscription tiers

**Verification Script**:
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
echo "=== Multi-Agent Prereq Check ==="

# Check Claude Code
claude --version && echo "✓ Claude Code" || echo "✗ Claude Code missing"

# Check Codex
codex --version && echo "✓ Codex" || echo "✗ Codex missing"

# Check Gemini CLI
gemini --version && echo "✓ Gemini CLI" || echo "✗ Gemini CLI missing"

# Check ntm
ntm --version && echo "✓ ntm" || echo "✗ ntm missing"

# Check brenner CLI
./brenner doctor

echo "=== End Check ==="
\`\`\`

**Troubleshooting Links**:
- Claude Code install: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
- Codex install: https://github.com/openai/codex-cli
- Gemini CLI install: [docs link]
- ntm install: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm

---

## Step 3: Start Agent Mail (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/3/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Clone/install Agent Mail
- Start the server
- Verify health

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Clone Agent Mail (if not already installed)
git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp-agent-mail.git
cd mcp-agent-mail

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Start the server
bun run start

# In another terminal, verify health
curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/health
# Expected: {"status":"ok","version":"0.x.x"}
\`\`\`

**Explanation**:
"Agent Mail provides the coordination bus. Agents post messages to threads, 
read their inbox, and acknowledge receipt. This enables asynchronous 
collaboration without agents stepping on each other."

---

## Step 4: Configure ntm (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/4/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Create an ntm session
- Understand pane structure
- Configure for three agents

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Create a new research session
export SESSION_ID="RS-$(date +%Y%m%d)-my-research"
ntm new $SESSION_ID

# Create panes for each agent
ntm split -n codex
ntm split -n claude
ntm split -n gemini

# Verify structure
ntm list
\`\`\`

**Session Layout**:
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pane 0: Control (you run commands here)     │
├──────────────┬──────────────┬───────────────┤
│ Pane 1:      │ Pane 2:      │ Pane 3:       │
│ codex        │ claude       │ gemini        │
│ (GPT 5.2)    │ (Opus 4.5)   │ (Gemini 3)    │
└──────────────┴──────────────┴───────────────┘
\`\`\`

---

## Step 5: Role Assignment (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/5/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Explain the three roles
- Map agents to roles
- Understand role-specific operators

**Role Definitions**:

**hypothesis_generator** (assigned to Codex/GPT):
- Primary operators: ⊘ Level-split, ⊕ Cross-domain, ◊ Paradox-hunt
- Produces: Hypothesis slate with 2-5 candidates + third alternative
- Key discipline: No mechanism = no hypothesis

**test_designer** (assigned to Claude/Opus):
- Primary operators: ✂ Exclusion-test, ⟂ Object-transpose, 🎭 Potency-check
- Produces: Discriminative tests that separate hypotheses
- Key discipline: Every test needs potency check

**adversarial_critic** (assigned to Gemini):
- Primary operators: ΔE Exception-quarantine, † Theory-kill, ⊞ Scale-check
- Produces: Attacks on framing + assumption challenges
- Key discipline: Attack the framing, not just the details

**Role Map for CLI**:
\`\`\`bash
--role-map "BlueLake=hypothesis_generator,PurpleMountain=test_designer,GreenValley=adversarial_critic"
\`\`\`

---

## Step 6: Build Research Context (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/6/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Search corpus for domain-relevant material
- Compile evidence pack (if external evidence)
- Build excerpt file
- Review all materials before kickoff

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Search corpus
./brenner corpus search "your topic" --limit 20

# Build excerpt
./brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161,... > excerpt.md

# Optional: Add external evidence
./brenner evidence add --file paper.pdf --anchor EV-001

# Review
cat excerpt.md
\`\`\`

---

## Step 7: Kick Off the Session (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/7/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Run the session start command
- Understand what happens (messages sent to Agent Mail)
- Verify kickoff succeeded

**The Big Command**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session start \\
  --project-key "$PWD" \\
  --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" \\
  --sender GreenCastle \\
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,GreenValley \\
  --role-map "BlueLake=hypothesis_generator,PurpleMountain=test_designer,GreenValley=adversarial_critic" \\
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \\
  --question "Your research question here"
\`\`\`

**What Happens**:
1. Three messages are sent to Agent Mail (one per role)
2. Each message has role-specific instructions
3. Agents will see messages in their inbox when they check

---

## Step 8: Run the Agents (~30 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/8/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Start each agent in its ntm pane
- Agents check inbox, see kickoff, respond
- Monitor progress

**In Each Pane**:
\`\`\`bash
# Pane 1 (codex)
codex --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"

# Pane 2 (claude)  
claude --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"

# Pane 3 (gemini)
gemini --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Each Agent Will**:
1. Check Agent Mail inbox
2. See the kickoff message for their role
3. Produce structured delta output
4. Post to the session thread

**Monitor Progress**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session status --thread-id "$SESSION_ID"
# Shows: which roles have responded, pending count
\`\`\`

---

## Step 9: Compile & Lint (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/9/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Wait for all roles to respond
- Compile the artifact from deltas
- Run linting to catch issues

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Wait for completion
./brenner session status --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" --watch

# Compile artifact
./brenner session compile --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" > artifact.json

# Lint for issues
./brenner lint artifact.json
\`\`\`

**Common Lint Errors**:
- Missing third alternative
- Test without potency check
- Assumption without scale check
- Hypothesis without mechanism

**Fix and Recompile**:
If lint errors, you can:
1. Ask a specific agent to revise
2. Manually edit the artifact
3. Post an intervention message

---

## Step 10: Iterate & Close (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/10/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Review the compiled artifact
- Optionally run a critique round
- Close the session
- Archive to research program

**Review the Artifact**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner artifact render --file artifact.json --format markdown
\`\`\`

**Critique Round (Optional)**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session critique \\
  --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" \\
  --artifact artifact.json
\`\`\`

**Close Session**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session close --thread-id "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Archive to Program**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner program add-session RP-MY-PROGRAM --session "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Checkpoint**: "You've run a full multi-agent Brenner Loop!"

---

# Testing Requirements

- Full E2E with all three agents
- Test on recommended hardware spec
- Test failure recovery (agent crashes mid-session)
- Test with multiple research domains

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 10 steps implemented with full content
- [ ] All CLI commands verified working
- [ ] Troubleshooting for each step
- [ ] Works with actual agent subscriptions
- [ ] Session artifacts compile correctly
- [ ] Lint catches real issues","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:53:14.4225605-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T22:59:03.712530583-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nm89","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.534424401-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

# Check Claude Code
claude --version && echo "✓ Claude Code" || echo "✗ Claude Code missing"

**Content Goals**:

**Commands**:

git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp-agent-mail.git

"Agent Mail provides the coordination bus. Agents post messages to threads, 
read their inbox, and acknowledge receipt. This enables asynchronous 
collaboration without agents stepping on each other."

---

## Step 4: Configure ntm (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/4/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Create an ntm session
- Understand pane structure
- Configure for three agents

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Create a new research session
export SESSION_ID="RS-$(date +%Y%m%d)-my-research"
ntm new $SESSION_ID

# Create panes for each agent
ntm split -n codex
ntm split -n claude
ntm split -n gemini

# Verify structure
ntm list
\`\`\`

**Session Layout**:
\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pane 0: Control (you run commands here)     │
├──────────────┬──────────────┬───────────────┤
│ Pane 1:      │ Pane 2:      │ Pane 3:       │
│ codex        │ claude       │ gemini        │
│ (GPT 5.2)    │ (Opus 4.5)   │ (Gemini 3)    │
└──────────────┴──────────────┴───────────────┘
\`\`\`

---

## Step 5: Role Assignment (~10 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/5/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Explain the three roles
- Map agents to roles
- Understand role-specific operators

**Role Definitions**:

**hypothesis_generator** (assigned to Codex/GPT):
- Primary operators: ⊘ Level-split, ⊕ Cross-domain, ◊ Paradox-hunt
- Produces: Hypothesis slate with 2-5 candidates + third alternative
- Key discipline: No mechanism = no hypothesis

**test_designer** (assigned to Claude/Opus):
- Primary operators: ✂ Exclusion-test, ⟂ Object-transpose, 🎭 Potency-check
- Produces: Discriminative tests that separate hypotheses
- Key discipline: Every test needs potency check

**adversarial_critic** (assigned to Gemini):
- Primary operators: ΔE Exception-quarantine, † Theory-kill, ⊞ Scale-check
- Produces: Attacks on framing + assumption challenges
- Key discipline: Attack the framing, not just the details

**Role Map for CLI**:
\`\`\`bash
--role-map "BlueLake=hypothesis_generator,PurpleMountain=test_designer,GreenValley=adversarial_critic"
\`\`\`

---

## Step 6: Build Research Context (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/6/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Search corpus for domain-relevant material
- Compile evidence pack (if external evidence)
- Build excerpt file
- Review all materials before kickoff

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Search corpus
./brenner corpus search "your topic" --limit 20

# Build excerpt
./brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161,... > excerpt.md

# Optional: Add external evidence
./brenner evidence add --file paper.pdf --anchor EV-001

# Review
cat excerpt.md
\`\`\`

---

## Step 7: Kick Off the Session (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/7/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Run the session start command
- Understand what happens (messages sent to Agent Mail)
- Verify kickoff succeeded

**The Big Command**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session start \\
  --project-key "$PWD" \\
  --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" \\
  --sender GreenCastle \\
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,GreenValley \\
  --role-map "BlueLake=hypothesis_generator,PurpleMountain=test_designer,GreenValley=adversarial_critic" \\
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \\
  --question "Your research question here"
\`\`\`

**What Happens**:
1. Three messages are sent to Agent Mail (one per role)
2. Each message has role-specific instructions
3. Agents will see messages in their inbox when they check

---

## Step 8: Run the Agents (~30 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/8/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Start each agent in its ntm pane
- Agents check inbox, see kickoff, respond
- Monitor progress

**In Each Pane**:
\`\`\`bash
# Pane 1 (codex)
codex --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"

# Pane 2 (claude)  
claude --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"

# Pane 3 (gemini)
gemini --project "$PWD" --session "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Each Agent Will**:
1. Check Agent Mail inbox
2. See the kickoff message for their role
3. Produce structured delta output
4. Post to the session thread

**Monitor Progress**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session status --thread-id "$SESSION_ID"
# Shows: which roles have responded, pending count
\`\`\`

---

## Step 9: Compile & Lint (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/9/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Wait for all roles to respond
- Compile the artifact from deltas
- Run linting to catch issues

**Commands**:
\`\`\`bash
# Wait for completion
./brenner session status --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" --watch

# Compile artifact
./brenner session compile --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" > artifact.json

# Lint for issues
./brenner lint artifact.json
\`\`\`

**Common Lint Errors**:
- Missing third alternative
- Test without potency check
- Assumption without scale check
- Hypothesis without mechanism

**Fix and Recompile**:
If lint errors, you can:
1. Ask a specific agent to revise
2. Manually edit the artifact
3. Post an intervention message

---

## Step 10: Iterate & Close (~15 min)
**File**: app/tutorial/multi-agent/10/page.tsx

**Content Goals**:
- Review the compiled artifact
- Optionally run a critique round
- Close the session
- Archive to research program

**Review the Artifact**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner artifact render --file artifact.json --format markdown
\`\`\`

**Critique Round (Optional)**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session critique \\
  --thread-id "$SESSION_ID" \\
  --artifact artifact.json
\`\`\`

**Close Session**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner session close --thread-id "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Archive to Program**:
\`\`\`bash
./brenner program add-session RP-MY-PROGRAM --session "$SESSION_ID"
\`\`\`

**Checkpoint**: "You've run a full multi-agent Brenner Loop!"

---

# Testing Requirements

- Full E2E with all three agents
- Test on recommended hardware spec
- Test failure recovery (agent crashes mid-session)
- Test with multiple research domains

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 10 steps implemented with full content
- [ ] All CLI commands verified working
- [ ] Troubleshooting for each step
- [ ] Works with actual agent subscriptions
- [ ] Session artifacts compile correctly
- [ ] Lint catches real issues","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:53:14.4225605-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:12:52.726710822-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:12:52.726710822-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all 10 tutorial steps for Multi-Agent Cockpit","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-nm89","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.534424401-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
